tier_intro: Let's start with the basics. This gives your AI agent the foundation it needs to represent your company accurately from day one. Takes about 3 minutes.

tier_complete.headline: Company identity locked in.
tier_complete.body: Your AI agent now knows who you are, what you do, and the market you serve. It can already introduce your company accurately to any customer. Next: give it your brand voice.

questions.question: What is your company's name?
questions.placeholder: Legal name or trading name — whatever customers know you as
questions.meta_profiling: How they write it reveals brand style — ALL CAPS, Title Case, lowercase brand like 'notion'. Note punctuation or special characters.
questions.why_this_matters: AI uses this exact name when introducing your company, in sign-offs, and whenever referring to your organization. Consistency matters.

questions.question: What industry are you in?
questions.meta_profiling: Industry selection sets baseline compliance needs, vocabulary register, and typical customer sophistication.
questions.why_this_matters: Industry shapes vocabulary, regulatory awareness, and the type of questions AI should expect. A healthcare AI speaks differently than a retail one.

questions.question: More specifically, what do you do? (optional)
questions.placeholder: e.g. 'B2B SaaS for logistics companies' or 'family-run bakery and catering'
questions.meta_profiling: How specific and confident they are in their sub-industry description. Jargon used reveals target audience sophistication.
questions.why_this_matters: Sub-industry context lets AI use precise terminology and avoid generic industry language that feels off-brand.

questions.question: How many people work at your company?
questions.meta_profiling: Company size affects how formal processes are, how much the AI needs to handle vs. route to specialists, and whether there are likely compliance teams.
questions.why_this_matters: A 3-person startup wants the AI to handle more. A 200-person company needs clear escalation paths. This shapes how autonomous the AI should be.

questions.question: What year was the company founded?
questions.meta_profiling: Age of company. Under 3 years = startup dynamics. Over 10 years = established processes, possibly formal culture.
questions.why_this_matters: AI can mention company history authentically. A 20-year-old company signals stability; a 2-year-old signals innovation.

questions.question: Where is your company based?
questions.meta_profiling: Location affects timezone awareness, local references, and cultural tone calibration.
questions.why_this_matters: AI can reference your timezone, use local currency and date formats, and avoid culturally mismatched references.

questions.question: Which markets do you serve?
questions.meta_profiling: Geographic scope reveals compliance requirements (GDPR if EU), language needs, and customer diversity expectations.
questions.why_this_matters: If you serve EU customers, AI needs GDPR awareness. Global reach means AI should handle time zone and language diversity gracefully.

questions.question: What stage best describes your company right now?

options.label: Startup — still figuring things out, moving fast
Growing — processes being built, scaling up
Established — stable, predictable, focused on efficiency

questions.meta_profiling: Stage affects formality, process maturity, and how much flexibility AI should have in handling edge cases.
questions.why_this_matters: A startup AI should be nimble and resourceful. An enterprise AI needs stricter guardrails and escalation paths.

questions.question: What values does your company stand for? Pick what genuinely fits.

options.label: Expertise — we know our field deeply
Personalisation — we treat every customer differently

questions.custom_placeholder: Add a value not listed here
questions.meta_profiling: Values chosen reveal authentic positioning vs. aspirational positioning. If all 10 are selected, likely aspirational. 3-4 specific values = genuine.
questions.why_this_matters: AI weaves these values into how it responds. A 'transparency' company never gives vague non-answers. A 'speed' company never makes customers wait without acknowledgment.

questions.question: What is your company's mission or purpose? (in your own words)
questions.placeholder: What problem do you solve? Why do you exist? Even a rough sentence is useful.
questions.meta_profiling: How they articulate this reveals clarity of company identity. Vague = brand positioning work needed. Sharp = confident founder/team.
questions.why_this_matters: When customers ask 'what do you actually do?' or 'why should I trust you?', this is the answer AI draws from. A clear mission builds trust fast.

questions.question: Do you have brand guidelines, a company deck, or an About page we can scan?
questions.placeholder: Upload any brand document — we'll extract what's useful automatically
questions.meta_profiling: Having polished brand docs = established marketing function. Absence = earlier stage or opportunity to generate docs.
questions.why_this_matters: A single brand doc can auto-fill dozens of questions across all tiers — saving you 20+ minutes of manual input.

questions.question: What is your company website URL?
questions.meta_profiling: Website quality, copy style, and product messaging all feed into later tiers automatically.
questions.why_this_matters: We'll scan your site to pre-fill brand voice, product descriptions, and FAQs — so you don't have to type what you've already written.
